claim: Cross-check confirms: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements harness boundary audit via file snapshot diffing that blocks PRD readiness when full-tool Claude agents mutate project files outside allowed artifact roots.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot() and diffBoundarySnapshots() implement SHA-256 digest comparison to detect added, modified, or deleted files. Allowed write roots are excluded from snapshot.
evidence.why: Core boundary audit detection mechanism verified independently.

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime() captures boundary snapshot before agent execution and compares after execution, detecting mutations.
evidence.why: Integration point where boundary audit is triggered during evidence agent runs.

evidence.summary: Boundary violations are converted to BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers and merged into readiness, forcing prd_ready=false.
evidence.why: Blocking mechanism that prevents PRD readiness on mutation detection.

evidence.summary: executionConfig() normalizes agent_tool_profile for Claude provider, applying 'research', 'full', or 'boundary' profiles with allowFullAgentTools gate.
evidence.why: Tool profile configuration layer that controls full-tool agent availability.

evidence.summary: Claude provider normalizes agent_tool_profile to 'research', 'full' (with allowFullAgentTools), or 'boundary' default, clearing tool restriction flags.
evidence.why: Provider adapter that enforces tool profile boundaries for Claude agents.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS define explorer, cross-checker, and verifier roles with writes_code=false and explicit protocols.
evidence.why: Schema layer defining evidence agent constraints.

evidence.summary: Test 'boundary blocks project file mutation even when agent results pass' verifies BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers prevent PRD readiness.
evidence.why: Test evidence confirming blocking behavior overrides agent results.

evidence.summary: Test 'claude full tool profile requires explicit authorization' verifies full profile only activates with allowFullAgentTools=true.
evidence.why: Test evidence confirming full-tool mode requires explicit authorization gate.

evidence.summary: Test 'git-backed boundary detects untracked project mutations' verifies git ls-files detects mutations including untracked files.
evidence.why: Test evidence confirming boundary works with git repositories.

evidence.summary: Test 'non-git boundary fallback detects modified project files and ignores artifacts' verifies filesystem walking detects mutations while excluding allowed_write_roots.
evidence.why: Test evidence confirming non-git boundary detection with artifact exclusion.

risks: Boundary snapshot may have false positives if temp/cache files are not properly excluded, causing legitimate runs to be blocked.
Boundary snapshot may have false negatives for large binary files, git submodules, or symlinks, allowing mutations to escape detection.
Transient mutations within the execution window (mutate and revert) would not be detected by boundary audit.
Concurrent filesystem changes from other processes during evidence execution could be incorrectly attributed to agents.

result.notes: All components of the explorer claim are independently verified: boundary audit implementation exists (snapshot diffing), PRD blocking is enforced (blockers prevent readiness), full-tool agent controls are in place (provider normalization and authorization gates), and tests confirm the blocking behavior. The multi-layer approach (agent instructions + post-execution audit + test coverage) provides defense-in-depth for full-tool agent scenarios.
